source_title: #949 - Jocko Willink - How To Build Unstoppable Confidence

paragraphs.speaker: jocko
paragraphs.name: Jocko Willink
paragraphs.text: I'm a fan of the Thomas Sowell quote. There are no solutions only trade-offs. That means you're never gonna completely solve anything in your life or in the world and there's always gonna be compromise that you have to make. You can't just do everything at once. You just have to figure out what the priority is. At certain times certain things might be a higher priority right now than it is at some other point and something else picks up and sometimes the family needs to be the priority sometimes the business sometimes the health like you're just gonna have to weigh those things out.

paragraphs.speaker: jocko
paragraphs.name: Jocko Willink
paragraphs.text: It's one of those Laws of Combat that I teach and used to teach in SEAL teams and now I teach the businesses. Prioritize and execute like you've got to figure out what the priorities are and then execute on those things. You got to figure out what the biggest priority is at any given time. Whether it's this business or your family or your health at certain times. That's the fourth law of combat leadership which is decentralized command meaning you can't do everything yourself you need to allow your subordinate leaders to step up and lead and make things happen.

paragraphs.speaker: jocko
paragraphs.name: Jocko Willink
paragraphs.text: You've got to be able to detach a little bit and take a step back and take a look at what's going on in the world and take a look at what's going on in your world. If you don't take a step back and you don't detach then you're not going to recognize what's happening what's going on. That's how someone can work until they have no family left or pay so much attention their family that they lose their job. Health is one that you don't want to totally walk away from at any time. I probably haven't taken more than two or three days off from working out in decades.

paragraphs.speaker: jocko
paragraphs.name: Jocko Willink
paragraphs.text: I think confidence comes from experience but I think one of the most profound things that you can do to become more confident is to become okay with saying yeah I'm not exactly sure what to do right now. If you can say that all of a sudden the world becomes just a lot easier. No one knows everything. No one makes all perfect decisions. I think a good way to gain confidence quickly is to admit the fact that you don't know everything and and just be okay with saying yeah I'm not really sure what to do here. That's liberating. I think it's very liberating.

paragraphs.speaker: jocko
paragraphs.name: Jocko Willink
paragraphs.text: When you're in the military you can be in charge of people that have more experience than you and if that makes you uncomfortable oftentimes people flex to try and act like they know more than they do. Instead you say hey Chris you've run these kind of operations before why don't you take lead on this? Does your respect for me go down or does it go up? It goes up exactly as opposed to me saying here's what we're gonna do Chris and I don't know what I'm talking about and you can clearly see this because you've done it 25 times. I would say be more humble and you'll be more confident.

paragraphs.speaker: jocko
paragraphs.name: Jocko Willink
paragraphs.text: Go. Yep. That's the answer to fear. It's when all that fear that's in your head all the what ifs and the scenarios that you create inside your own mind that are way worse than anything that is actually gonna happen they all disappear when you go. So just take action and start moving forward. That is the moment that can last a million years is waiting to go. Once you like stop get out of the vehicle all those things go away. Action is an antidote to all kinds of problems.

paragraphs.speaker: jocko
paragraphs.name: Jocko Willink
paragraphs.text: I think it's trained because you go through situations. In the military they're probably doing it to you on purpose. You're gonna go rappelling then you're gonna fast rope then you're gonna rappel off of a building then out of a helicopter then you're gonna free fall parachute. All those things there are all things that you can be afraid of. The longer that you sit you hang on and look and stare and contemplate the chances of you falling off it doesn't get any better. So eventually you just throw your leg over and get over that damn thing.
